TLK NYSE ADR is liquid: ~1.48M shares/day x ~$13.67 = ~$20M ADV, free float 471.6M sh (47.7%), $13.5B mkt cap — an equity-only expression is easily filled (staging/undated -> equity as the playbook prescribes). But the underlying expression mismatch is fatal: TLK ADR holders do NOT receive the spin entity. InfraNexia is a SUBSIDIARY carve-out of fiber assets, not a pro-rata distribution to TLK shareholders, so buying the ADR is not a clean claim on the catalyst.

cro.driver_confirmed: Spin_index_flow is MIS-TAGGED for current state. There is no listed spin and no pending index inclusion — InfraNexia is a private, pre-IPO subsidiary. Index flow only becomes a driver after an InfraNexia IPO is priced and an index add is scheduled; neither exists. Reads more like 'pre-spin restructuring narrative' than an actionable index-flow event. No second name in this single-name batch shares the driver.

picks.entry_rationale: Clean cash-merger arb at ~US$23.40 vs C$44.00 (~US$30.98 at live FX) = +32% gross spread on the only non-bio, non-FDA driver available (Foreign_regulator, brand-new to the basket), resolving on host-country regulatory approval rather than the tape. Sized moderate, not hero, because the downside floor cushion is ~zero (pure deal-break risk) and ADV is thin at ~$3.8M/day.
picks.invalidation: Deal breaks (financing/regulatory failure) or a definitive termination — price collapses toward standalone gold-tape value below the ~$23 floor with no cushion.
picks.review_trigger: July 29 2026 outside date for deal close / host-country approval (29d); a second mutual extension stretches the timeline rather than breaking the deal.

picks.entry_rationale: High-grade activist/sale-process bet (Jana) at $20.56 with +50.8% to FV $31 vs -19.7% to the $16.50 floor (R:R 2.57), a brand-new Activist_process driver in Idiosyncratic that diversifies away from the FDA-heavy held book. Staging=true (undated process) forces equity-only at half a normal weight; entry is if anything cheaper than dossier asof after today's -6.6%.
picks.invalidation: Jana stands down / board rejects a sale and reaffirms standalone, or a leveraged-balance-sheet stress event re-rates the equity toward/through the $16.50 floor.
picks.review_trigger: 2027-03-31 process milestone (274d); interim — any board strategic-review update or formal sale-process announcement.

passed.passed_because: Skip on merit regardless of headroom: thinnest R:R in the batch (~1.35) and only M grade — shallowest asymmetry — plus an active ZYME.TEN/ZYME.EXC tender/exchange corporate action that muddies the binary. Even if its FDA_approval_decision driver and the bio lane freed, the compressed edge does not earn a seat.

Undated open-ended binary (PRISM-MEL-301 Phase 3 with no fixed topline date) and a low 0.40 win-prob; no review milestone to anchor a position. Dateless low-conviction binary in a saturated lane — a merit skip, not just a headroom block.

Undated AND just-slipped milestone (VIASKIN Peanut BLA not yet filed, now anticipated Q3 2026+, review clock starts only at filing) on a deteriorating timeline, plus a thin ~$8M/day small-cap ADR. A demonstrably lengthening, dateless catalyst is a merit skip irrespective of cap headroom.

director.memo: Held book is FDA/biotech-saturated: both FDA drivers (approval, clinical-readout) and the entire bio_convergence lane (5/5) are full, so 5 of 7 survivors — all bio binaries (BBIO, EYPT, ZYME, IMCR, DBVT) — are hard-blocked. Only 2 names clear the combined caps, and they happen to be exactly the two cleanest diversifiers: AAUC (Foreign_regulator) and FUN (Activist_process) — both brand-new drivers, both non-bio, deliberately pulling the new seats AWAY from the FDA concentration into Deal-completion (29/40 after add) and Idiosyncratic (24/40). Shape: AAUC at 7% is a moderate cash-arb seat (huge headline R:R but ~zero floor cushion + thin ADV, so not oversized); FUN at 4% respects the staging=true / equity-only / half-weight rule and its 19.7% floor distance (risk-to-floor 0.79% << 1.5%). Watchlist holds the two H-grade, dated, headroom-only-blocked binaries (BBIO, EYPT) — first to seat when a held FDA name resolves and frees both a driver slot and a bio-lane slot. ZYME/IMCR/DBVT are merit passes (shallow / undated / undated-and-slipping). New seats 2; total seats 12/20; no cap breached.
